name: multimac
title: multimac

commands.name: multimac
commands.raw_help:
root@kali:~# multimac -h
 Usage: multimac <number of taps>

binary_metadata.file_type: ELF 64-bit LSB pie executable, x86-64, version 1 (SYSV), dynamically linked, interpreter /lib64/ld-linux-x86-64.so.2, BuildID[sha1]=0441fcc00f6cf6b9c2d09add1b1baa90b7180ad0, for GNU/Linux 3.2.0, stripped

apt_metadata.description:
Create multiple MACs on an adapter
Multimac is a Linux virtual ethernet tap allocator to
emulate and use multiple virtual interfaces (with different
MAC addresses) on a LAN using a single network adapter.

use_cases: When you need Kali Linux, use multimac because ### multimac.
When you need a concrete invocation, run `multimac <number of taps>` as documented upstream for multimac.
When chaining Kali Linux tools, pair multimac with sibling catalog entries sharing its attack_surface/phase_hint.
When validating lab setup, run `multimac --help` (or the README entrypoint) and confirm required hardware/deps from the catalog.

documentation.how_to_use: Install/setup: sudo apt install multimac. Run the primary entrypoint as shown in upstream docs, e.g. `multimac <number of taps>`.
documentation.when_to_use: Use during the 'any' phase against surfaces [web] in the Kali Linux category. Fits when the engagement needs: ### multimac.
usage_sections: Install with: sudo apt install multimac
Install with: sudo apt install multimac
